title: Executive Order 9761—Preservation and Display of Enemy Flags Captured by the Navy and the Coast Guard

flags.justification: A purely ceremonial and administrative housekeeping order regarding the disposition of captured war trophies.
A ceremonial directive, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing administrative practice, evergreen by nature.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A purely symbolic and administrative action.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Notable for superseding an order issued by President James K. Polk in 1849 — one of the longest-standing predecessor orders referenced anywhere in this corpus.
